channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Phoenix Suns
channel.description: Phoenix Suns trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Dillon Brooks on LeBron James: I don't think it's his last year, I think he has one more in him
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] “I don’t think it’s his last year,” Suns forward Dillon Brooks, who was in attendance, told Yahoo Sports. “I think he got one more in him. I watched him when I was in the NBA, when I was a kid in high school. He’s got a phenomenal career and battled everybody and done it at a high level throughout. It’s been amazing to watch overall, a pleasure to battle against him.” Yahoo! Sports

item.title: Dillon Brooks courtside and laughs at LeBron having …
item.description: Dillon Brooks courtside and laughs at LeBron having words for OKC after foul 😂

item.title: Some early winners include Michigan’s Aday Mara (7’3″ …
item.description: Prospects went through anthropometric measurements at the draft combine on Monday. The official measurements won’t be finalized until Tuesday, notes Jonathan Givony of DraftExpress (via Twitter), but some early winners include Michigan’s Aday Mara (7’3″ and 260 pounds with a 7’6″ wingspan and a 9’9″ standing reach, tied with Mark Williams for the second-longest in combine history), Jayden Quaintance and Chris Cenac, while Christian Anderson and Amari Allen were among the measurement losers, acco

item.title: Dillon Brooks courtside and laughs at LeBron having …
item.description: Dillon Brooks courtside and laughs at LeBron having words for OKC after foul 😂

item.title: Some early winners include Michigan’s Aday Mara (7’3″ …
item.description: Prospects went through anthropometric measurements at the draft combine on Monday. The official measurements won’t be finalized until Tuesday, notes Jonathan Givony of DraftExpress (via Twitter), but some early winners include Michigan’s Aday Mara (7’3″ and 260 pounds with a 7’6″ wingspan and a 9’9″ standing reach, tied with Mark Williams for the second-longest in combine history), Jayden Quaintance and Chris Cenac, while Christian Anderson and Amari Allen were among the measurement losers, acco

item.title: It’s hard to gauge whether the Rockets got the better …
item.description: How do you think the Kevin Durant trade played out for the Rockets? Was it worth losing players such as Jalen Green and Dillon Brooks? Metta World Peace: I think it’s played out the same way for both the Rockets and the Phoenix Suns. It was quite an even trade. It’s tough for the Rockets because Durant missed Game 1 with a knee injury, then injured his ankle in Game 2, causing him to miss more time. That hurt them a lot. I’ve been injured in the playoffs, so I know how it feels. I was supposed t

item.title: 76ers tried to make trades for Donte DiVincenzo, Naji Marshall, Ryan Dunn, Brandin Podziemski at deadline
item.description: Among other players the source said the Sixers made runs at, unsuccessfully: Naji Marshall of the Dallas Mavericks, Donte DiVincenzo of the Minnesota Timberwolves, Brandin Podziemski of the Golden State Warriors and Ryan Dunn of the Phoenix Suns. None of those players were moved.

item.title: Dillon Brooks: Once Wemby gets his first MVP, you won't see anybody else get one
item.description: Brooks spoke about Victor Wembanyama’s incredible talent, saying that once the French star reaches his peak and wins his first MVP award, we might not see another player claim the honor until he retires. "Once Wemby gets his first, you probably won't see anybody else get one," Brooks said. "His numbers are gonna go up."

item.title: Brooks tells Ray that Victor Wembanyama hasn't reached …
item.description: Slatt♱: Dillon Brooks tells Ray that Victor Wembanyama hasn't reached his pull potential yet & he's the only one in the NBA who can guard him

item.title: Darryn Peterson breaks down what he's learning from Devin Booker, Anthony Edwards, SGA, Donovan Mitchell and Dame Lillard
item.description: Q. Some film you're watching of guys that you're trying to emulate at the next level? Darryn Peterson: Devin Booker, Anthony Edwards, Shai,, Donovan Mitchell, and Dame. Q. What are you taking from them particularly? Peterson: Just ways that they score. They all score in different ways. Let's see how they take space and most of them are getting guarded super hard during their game. How they get easy shots, how they get to free throw line.

item.title: When I spoke to Darryn Peterson he had a very clear …
item.description: Ramona Shelburne: When I spoke to Darryn Peterson he had a very clear answer on what current player he admired: Devin Booker. Joined NBA Today to discuss with @king .mclure @marcjspears @MJAcostaTV #nba #nbatoday

item.title: "I'm going to throw a shot. I can't even lie. This guy …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Dillon Brooks called Jared Vanderbilt a ‘dumb NBA player’ "I'm going to throw a shot. I can't even lie. This guy doesn't even like me, to be honest with you. Jared Vanderbilt. On the Lakers. They call him Vando” (h/t @PurpGoldLakers)

item.title: Team and league sources told The Athletic that there …
item.description: Two years ago, Connelly was feverishly trying to line up a series of moves to make it possible for the Wolves to land Kevin Durant from Phoenix. Team and league sources told The Athletic that there was a point in the hours before the trade deadline in 2025 that the Wolves believed they were in position to execute, but the Suns ultimately decided not to pull the trigger.

item.title: Western Conference Finals game is going to double …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: A Western Conference Finals game is going to double overtime for the first time since 1976 (Game 4 of Warriors/Suns).

item.title: 11 coaches have made the NBA Finals since 2020
item.description: Tim Reynolds: 11 coaches have made the NBA Finals since 2020. 6 are no longer with the teams they took to those Finals. 2024: Jason Kidd, Dallas 2023: Michael Malone, Denver 2022: Ime Udoka, Boston 2021: Monty Williams, Phoenix 2021: Mike Budenholzer, Milwaukee 2020: Frank Vogel, Lakers
